_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.

vulnerability_fixed.description: Command injection via prefix matching in execute.cgi
vulnerability_fixed.fix: Removed prefix matching from whitelist check - now exact match only

cgi_security.execute_cgi: Hardened - exact whitelist match only (prefix match vulnerability fixed 2026-03-26)
cgi_security.terminal_cgi: Safe - case statement with hardcoded commands, argument sanitization
cgi_security.action_cgi: Safe - delegates to queue script with rate limiting
cgi_security.prompt_version_cgi: Safe - agent whitelist and hex-only hash validation

known_issues: World-writable API files (action-queue.json, action-status.json, postmortems.json) - required for CGI, mitigated by whitelist validation
tasks.md in webroot intentionally exposed for task viewer feature - contains non-sensitive task data only
Git history contains pattern matches for secret-like keywords - audit confirms these are search patterns in scripts, not actual credentials
notes: FIXED: Critical command injection vulnerability in execute.cgi - prefix matching allowed appending malicious commands after whitelisted prefixes
Log rotation occurred since last review - current auth.log covers 2026-03-22 to present
Total failed SSH attempts: 77,214 (up from 63,472 at last review on 2026-01-25)
Top attacker changed: 165.154.6.42 now leads with 2,417 attempts
All nginx protections verified - all sensitive file types return 404
Path traversal blocked (direct returns 404, URL-encoded returns 400)
SSH key permissions correct (600), SSH dir correct (700)
No eval() calls found in JavaScript files
4 CGI scripts reviewed: execute.cgi, terminal.cgi, action.cgi, prompt-version.cgi
Disk usage at 12% (up from 7% at last review)
recommendations: CRITICAL: Install fail2ban for SSH protection - 77k+ total attacks from 739+ unique IPs
Enable UFW firewall to restrict unnecessary ports
Consider rate limiting on web endpoints
